title: between the pulses
essence: A procedural night sky that develops only for those who remain still — constellations form, name themselves, and a transit traces a poem for the patient witness.
description: An eyepiece, not a screen. The viewer is given a deep-field void seeded with hundreds of stars whose light has not yet arrived. Stillness draws them out; constant motion fogs the optic and resets the field. After ninety seconds of patience a constellation names itself, and at one hundred fifty seconds a transit scrolls a four-line poem in the shadow of a passing world.
interaction_summary: Remain still and watch — the more patiently you witness, the deeper the sky develops; move continuously and the eyepiece fogs and the field resets.
secrets: After 90 seconds of cumulative witnessing (and a few seconds of held stillness), a brightened constellation labels itself: 'the listener'.
After 150 seconds, a planet transits horizontally and its shadow trails a four-line poem about parsecs and patience.
The starfield is generated from a fixed seed for tonight — every still witness sees the same sky, like a real constellation.
Bright stars carry a faint radial halo whose intensity scales with their inferred magnitude — only visible when the optic is unfogged.

wake_log.event: First inkling: a star map should not greet you. It should make you earn arrival. The eyepiece as a contract of stillness.
The reset nearly killed it. Hard cuts felt punitive. Replaced with a slow fog and a phrase — 'the field has clouded' — and the field pulses back as soon as the witness settles.

Kept: the seeded sky, the transit poem, the named constellation, the red flashlight. Abandoned: a soundtrack of pulsar clicks (too loud, too literal); and a 'name your own constellation' input (too participatory — this is a meditation, not a craft).
